content: Hi! I'm currently trying to import one of the DRM header files, include/uapi/drm/drm_fourcc.h, into rust. It's just a long list of various "fourcc" codes which are just a bunch of u32s that represent different color formats. I was mainly wondering if there's any way I could do this in an automated fashion, since by default it doesn't seem like bindgen picks up any of the macros that are defined using the fourcc_code() macro. Is there any way I could still easily automate this with bindgen without having to redefine all of the values there on the rust side?
For userspace usage there is https://github.com/dzfranklin/drm-fourcc-rs which uses a bunch of regexes on the header files.

@Lyude Not possible as usual due to bindgen #753. Ask chatGPT or Github Copilot or whatever to convert a header file with some constants and macros into Rust for you and it will do so after some massaging though. Saves you from writing a bunch of regexes by hand.
(well, in this particular case your issue is sorted by someone else, but the tip may prove itself useful in the future regardless)
oh YES woo thank you for linking this, I wonder if we can get this into the main rust branch we've been trying to make
oh wait I just noticed that's a userspasce library haha, but still that's good to know

Daniel Almeida said:

Lyude Not possible as usual due to bindgen #753. Ask chatGPT or Github Copilot or whatever to convert a header file with some constants and macros into Rust for you and it will do so after some massaging though. Saves you from writing a bunch of regexes by hand.

I don't generally think we should be using tools that encourage people to use stuff like chatgpt to send us low effort patches thta make us put more effort into reviewing for possibly no return so - I'm going to stay away from that, but thank you for the advice ♥
I feel like I remember seeing a proposed hostprog that did this, don't know what happened to it
Or maybe it was just a perl one liner, same difference :smile:
Maybe drm_fourcc.hshould be generated from JSON or similar?
